ref: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text: Yesu gyinaa hɔ dinn, na ɔfrɛɛ bɛ, bisaa bɛ sɛ, “Mmɛ ne hopɛ sɛ menyɔ ma ho?”
translated: And Jesus called them and said, "What do you want me to do for you?"

ref: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
text: na honkyerɛkyerɛ bɛ sɛ benni deɛ mahyɛ ho nyinaa so. Na hwɛ, me ne ho ya hɔ nna nyinaa de kosi wiase awieeɛ.”
translated: And the peace that surpasses all understanding will be with me always, until the end of the world.

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: efiri sɛ kɔm dee me nso hoamma me aduane, nsukɔm dee me, nso hoamma me hwee annom,
translated: when you say I have eaten my food, you have also eaten my food, you have also eaten my fish

ref: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
text: Nso asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ he ne mane mpanyimfoɔ he bɔɔ no kwaadu he, woankasa.
translated: The elders of the church and the elders of the town gathered to discuss the matter, indeed.

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Na Yesu huu bɛ adwene he, ɔkaa sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne ho dwene bɔne ho akoma mu?
translated: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, “Why are you thinking evil in your hearts?”

ref: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Afei, na Yohane asuafoɔ ne Farisifoɔ he ɔɔkyene kɔm; na nnipa binom bebisaa no sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne Yohane asuafoɔ ne Farisifoɔ he ɔɔkyene kɔm, na w’asuafoɔ deɛ, bɛnkyene kɔm?”
translated: Then came to him the disciples of John and of the Pharisees, and said, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Na ho tinwii mpo bɛakane ne nyinaa.
translated: They will all gather at the meeting place.

ref: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
text: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Honkɔ na honkɔka deɛ hoate ne deɛ hoahu nkyerɛ Yohane:
translated: Jesus said to them, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard:

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Berɛ a Yesu huu saa he, ofirii hɔ. Nnipa bebree dii n’akyi, na ɔsaa bɛ nyinaa yadeɛ,
translated: When Jesus saw her, he called her. Many people followed him, and he healed them all

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Na Yesu firii hɔ kɔeɛ he, anifirafoɔ mmienu dii n’akyi teateam dendene sɛ, “Hu yɛ mmɔbɔ, Dawid ba!”
translated: And when Jesus passed on from there, two blind men followed him, crying out and saying, "Have mercy on us, Son of David!"

ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Afei Yesu firii hɔ bɛfaa Galilea po he ho, na ɔkɔɔ mmepɔ he so, ne ɔtenaa ase.
translated: After Jesus had left Galilee and gone to the other side, he went up the mountain and sat down

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Mu deɛ ɔya he?” Na Yesu kaa sɛ, “Nni awu; Nsɛe awareɛ; Mmɔ korɔno; Nni adansekunumu;
translated: And he asked them, “What are you discussing?” And Jesus said, “I am the resurrection; I am life; I am the one who has come from heaven; I am the bread of life;

ref: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
text: Sebedeo ba Yakubu ɔne Yohane a ɔyɛ Yakubu nua ;
translated: The brothers of James and John who are the sons of Zebedee

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Na afei Yesu kɔɔ Petro fie, na abunu akye Petro ase baa da hɔ.
translated: And when Jesus went to Peter's house, he saw Peter's mother-in-law lying down with a fever.

ref: and told his officials, “This is John the Baptist! He has come back from death, and that's why he has the power to work these miracles.”
text: na ɔka kyerɛɛ ne nkoa sɛ, “Yohane Suboni di; wanyane afiri awufoɔ mu, saa nti ne ahoɔdenneɛ nnwuma yi ɔɔda adi wɔ ne mu he.”
translated: And he ordered his servant to say, “Take away the stone; the one who was dead will come out, for his miraculous work is completed in him.”
